title: ICE Illegally Scooped Up Medicaid Data, Then Shared It With Palantir
excerpt: The Trump administration’s continual trend towards maximum awfulness means that every report seems to be “I’ve got bad news and I’ve got worse news.” What was already …
content_html:
from the acceptable-collateral-damage dept

The Trump administration’s continual trend towards maximum awfulness means that every report seems to be “I’ve got bad news and I’ve got worse news.” What was already bad has become even worse now that more of the administration’s actions have been exposed during court proceedings.

Last July, the Trump administration unilaterally decided ICE should have access to Medicaid data for the sole purpose of locating migrants to arrest and deport. That much was made clear by the administration itself, which said the data would give ICE officials better tools to discover “the location of aliens.” An agreement was reached with the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Service by the DHS because of course that happened. The administration had already purged plenty of non-loyalists, which meant those remaining wouldn’t put up much of fight.

There was no legal basis for this demand, which is why the headline says “illegally.” If this was a legal request, we wouldn’t be seeing lawsuits challenging the sharing of this sensitive medical data because the law would already be settled. Specifically, ICE wanted access to Medicaid data that exposed “home addresses and ethnicities.” Not exactly subtle, but nothing ever is with this grotesque shotgun of an administration.

But this sharing was challenged in court, and that converts that bad news to worse news. Not only did ICE have access to information it wasn’t legally entitled to have, but its private contractors did too, as NPR reports:

After Medicaid officials improperly shared data about millions of people in January with immigration officials, ICE then shared that data with the data analytics firm Palantir, according to new court filings. Palantir operates an app called ELITE that is used by ICE agents to show the addresses of noncitizens who may be subject to deportation.

That revelation was made public in a motion filed Thursday by more than 20 Democratic attorneys general who sued the Trump administration last year over its data-sharing agreement between the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services and ICE.

Palantir’s thirst for data is constant. And it will take anything its government customers choose to give it, including information that has been obtained illegally.

Palantir issued a couple of statements in response to this reporting based on courtroom revelations in ongoing lawsuits.

First, it said that the data in question had been “purged.” That’s great if true, but this seems like something that needs verification before trust because who knows where else this data set ended up before court orders blocked the government from using this data. If you think only ICE was peeking into this illicitly obtained data, you’re awarding the government the good faith it not only hasn’t earned, but has spent pretty much every minute since Trump’s election actively destroying.

ICE’s surveillance tech contractor also said this:

Palantir provided the following statement to NPR: “Our customers control their own data and manage access to that data. When Palantir employees are granted access to a customer’s dataset, it is solely to help integrate and analyze that data — which is what our software does — not to store it or use it for our own purposes. Palantir can confirm that the dataset in question was purged pursuant to government instruction.”

Well… I’d like to believe this much in the same way I’d like to believe a system of checks and balances is capable of constraining a rogue regime, much less its private contractors who are not subject to these particular restraints.

I believe Palantir to the extent that its employees aren’t just surfing waves of incoming data for their own personal reasons, but I find it extremely difficult to believe that a belated “purge” has actually scrubbed the data and removed any ancillary… well, let’s call them “infections.” Without turning over evidence of this purge to the courts, it’s easy to say it’s all been handled, even if the only thing that happened what Palantir deleting the source CSVs (or whatever) from its system, which isn’t the same thing as stripping it from Palantir’s databases.

Another reason for high levels of skepticism is this: ICE somehow couldn’t stop itself from passing this illegally obtained data to Palantir despite (apparently) trying to comply with a court order.

In a court filing last week, the Justice Department said that CMS again inadvertently reshared with ICE the dataset with millions of names that CMS had first improperly shared with ICE in January. The government said the error occurred during an effort to share data from states not involved in the lawsuit.

You see the problem, right? Because not every state sued over this illegal data collection, ICE continues to collect data that should — at this point — be considered off-limits. The only reason it doesn’t is because some states (you know the ones) have decided they’ll do whatever it takes to ensure the administration gets to keep being openly racist.

Consequently, the data sets aren’t being sorted between racist/non-racist (or whatever the SORT term is), which means ICE continues to retain data it’s been ordered to delete and Palantir keeps getting handed data the government isn’t allowed to collect, much less distribute.

Then there’s the ultimate problem. No matter what’s happening here in the lower courts, the administration will continue to push for a resolution from the US Supreme Court. And the odds are about 6-3 that SCOTUS will say the government can do whatever it wants with whatever data it collects, ignoring years of precedent and administrative firewalls that are meant to protect US citizens (and residents) from being abused and surveilled by their government.

Filed Under: dhs, ice, mass deportation, medicaid, medical data, trump administration
Companies: palantir

sentences.en: from the acceptable-collateral-damage dept
sentences.zh: 来自可接受的附带损害部门

blocks.html_en: The Trump administration’s continual trend towards maximum awfulness means that every report seems to be “I’ve got bad news and I’ve got worse news.” What was already bad has become even worse now that more of the administration’s actions have been exposed during court proceedings.

sentences.en: The Trump administration’s continual trend towards maximum awfulness means that every report seems to be “I’ve got bad news and I’ve got worse news.”
sentences.zh: 特朗普政府持续走向最糟糕的趋势意味着每一份报告似乎都是“我得到了坏消息，我得到了更糟糕的消息”。

sentences.en: What was already bad has become even worse now that more of the administration’s actions have been exposed during court proceedings.
sentences.zh: 由于政府的更多行为在法庭诉讼中曝光，本来就很糟糕的情况变得更加糟糕。

blocks.html_en: Last July, the Trump administration unilaterally decided ICE should have access to Medicaid data for the sole purpose of locating migrants to arrest and deport. That much was made clear by the administration itself, which said the data would give ICE officials better tools to discover “the location of aliens.” An agreement was reached with the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Service by the DHS because of course that happened. The administration had already purged plenty of non-loyalists, which meant those remaining wouldn’t put up much of fight.

sentences.en: Last July, the Trump administration unilaterally decided ICE should have access to Medicaid data for the sole purpose of locating migrants to arrest and deport.
sentences.zh: 去年 7 月，特朗普政府单方面决定 ICE 应该获取医疗补助数据，其唯一目的是寻找移民以进行逮捕和驱逐。

sentences.en: That much was made clear by the administration itself, which said the data would give ICE officials better tools to discover “the location of aliens.”
sentences.zh: 政府本身已经明确表示了这一点，并表示这些数据将为 ICE 官员提供更好的工具来发现“外星人的位置”。

sentences.en: An agreement was reached with the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Service by the DHS because of course that happened.
sentences.zh: 当然，国土安全部与医疗保险和医疗补助服务中心达成协议，因为这种情况发生了。

sentences.en: The administration had already purged plenty of non-loyalists, which meant those remaining wouldn’t put up much of fight.
sentences.zh: 政府已经清除了大量的非效忠派，这意味着剩下的人不会进行太多的战斗。

blocks.html_en: There was no legal basis for this demand, which is why the headline says “illegally.” If this was a legal request, we wouldn’t be seeing lawsuits challenging the sharing of this sensitive medical data because the law would already be settled. Specifically, ICE wanted access to Medicaid data that exposed “home addresses and ethnicities.” Not exactly subtle, but nothing ever is with this grotesque shotgun of an administration.

sentences.en: There was no legal basis for this demand, which is why the headline says “illegally.”
sentences.zh: 这种要求没有法律依据，所以标题上写着“非法”。

sentences.en: If this was a legal request, we wouldn’t be seeing lawsuits challenging the sharing of this sensitive medical data because the law would already be settled.
sentences.zh: 如果这是一个法律要求，我们就不会看到挑战共享这些敏感医疗数据的诉讼，因为法律已经解决了。

sentences.en: Specifically, ICE wanted access to Medicaid data that exposed “home addresses and ethnicities.”
sentences.zh: 具体来说，ICE 希望获得暴露“家庭住址和种族”的医疗补助数据。

sentences.en: Not exactly subtle, but nothing ever is with this grotesque shotgun of an administration.
sentences.zh: 不完全是微妙的，但对于这支怪诞的政府来说，一切都不是那么回事。

sentences.en: But this sharing was challenged in court, and that converts that bad news to worse news.
sentences.zh: 但这种分享在法庭上受到了质疑，这让坏消息变成了更糟糕的消息。

sentences.en: Not only did ICE have access to information it wasn’t legally entitled to have, but its private contractors did too, as NPR reports:
sentences.zh: 据 NPR 报道，ICE 不仅可以获取其在法律上无权获取的信息，而且其私人承包商也可以获取：

blocks.html_en:
After Medicaid officials improperly shared data about millions of people in January with immigration officials, ICE then shared that data with the data analytics firm Palantir, according to new court filings. Palantir operates an app called ELITE that is used by ICE agents to show the addresses of noncitizens who may be subject to deportation.

That revelation was made public in a motion filed Thursday by more than 20 Democratic attorneys general who sued the Trump administration last year over its data-sharing agreement between the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services and ICE.

sentences.en: After Medicaid officials improperly shared data about millions of people in January with immigration officials, ICE then shared that data with the data analytics firm Palantir, according to new court filings.
sentences.zh: 根据新的法庭文件，在医疗补助官员一月份与移民官员不当分享了数百万人的数据后，ICE 随后与数据分析公司 Palantir 分享了这些数据。

sentences.en: Palantir operates an app called ELITE that is used by ICE agents to show the addresses of noncitizens who may be subject to deportation.
sentences.zh: Palantir 运营一款名为 ELITE 的应用程序，ICE 特工使用该应用程序来显示可能被驱逐出境的非公民的地址。

sentences.en: That revelation was made public in a motion filed Thursday by more than 20 Democratic attorneys general who sued the Trump administration last year over its data-sharing agreement between the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services and ICE.
sentences.zh: 周四，20 多名民主党总检察长提交的一项动议公开了这一消息，他们去年就医疗保险和医疗补助服务中心与 ICE 之间的数据共享协议起诉特朗普政府。

blocks.html_en: After Medicaid officials improperly shared data about millions of people in January with immigration officials, ICE then shared that data with the data analytics firm Palantir, according to new court filings. Palantir operates an app called ELITE that is used by ICE agents to show the addresses of noncitizens who may be subject to deportation.

blocks.html_en: Palantir’s thirst for data is constant. And it will take anything its government customers choose to give it, including information that has been obtained illegally.

sentences.en: Palantir’s thirst for data is constant.
sentences.zh: Palantir 对数据的渴望始终如一。

sentences.en: And it will take anything its government customers choose to give it, including information that has been obtained illegally.
sentences.zh: 它会接受政府客户选择提供的任何信息，包括非法获得的信息。

blocks.html_en: Palantir issued a couple of statements in response to this reporting based on courtroom revelations in ongoing lawsuits.

sentences.en: Palantir issued a couple of statements in response to this reporting based on courtroom revelations in ongoing lawsuits.
sentences.zh: Palantir 根据正在进行的诉讼中法庭披露的内容，针对本报道发表了几份声明。

blocks.html_en: First, it said that the data in question had been “purged.” That’s great if true, but this seems like something that needs verification before trust because who knows where else this data set ended up before court orders blocked the government from using this data. If you think only ICE was peeking into this illicitly obtained data, you’re awarding the government the good faith it not only hasn’t earned, but has spent pretty much every minute since Trump’s election actively destroying.

sentences.en: First, it said that the data in question had been “purged.”
sentences.zh: 首先，它表示相关数据已被“清除”。

sentences.en: That’s great if true, but this seems like something that needs verification before trust because who knows where else this data set ended up before court orders blocked the government from using this data.
sentences.zh: 如果这是真的那就太好了，但这似乎在信任之前需要验证，因为谁知道在法院命令阻止政府使用这些数据之前，这些数据集最终去了哪里。

sentences.en: If you think only ICE was peeking into this illicitly obtained data, you’re awarding the government the good faith it not only hasn’t earned, but has spent pretty much every minute since Trump’s election actively destroying.
sentences.zh: 如果你认为只有 ICE 正在窥视这些非法获取的数据，那么你就给予了政府良好的信誉，但政府不仅没有赢得这种诚意，而且自特朗普当选以来几乎每一分钟都在积极破坏。

Palantir can confirm that the dataset in question was purged pursuant to government instruction.”

sentences.zh: 当 Palantir 员工被授予访问客户数据集的权限时，只是为了帮助集成和分析该数据（这就是我们软件的作用），而不是为了我们自己的目的而存储或使用它。

sentences.en: Palantir can confirm that the dataset in question was purged pursuant to government instruction.”
sentences.zh: Palantir 可以确认相关数据集已根据政府指示被清除。”

sentences.zh: 出色地…

sentences.en: I’d like to believe this much in the same way I’d like to believe a system of checks and balances is capable of constraining a rogue regime, much less its private contractors who are not subject to these particular restraints.
sentences.zh: 我愿意相信这一点，就像我愿意相信制衡体系能够限制流氓政权一样，更不用说不受这些特定限制的私人承包商了。

blocks.html_en: I believe Palantir to the extent that its employees aren’t just surfing waves of incoming data for their own personal reasons, but I find it extremely difficult to believe that a belated “purge” has actually scrubbed the data and removed any ancillary… well, let’s call them “infections.” Without turning over evidence of this purge to the courts, it’s easy to say it’s all been handled, even if the only thing that happened what Palantir deleting the source CSVs (or whatever) from its system, which isn’t the same thing as stripping it from Palantir’s databases.

sentences.en: I believe Palantir to the extent that its employees aren’t just surfing waves of incoming data for their own personal reasons, but I find it extremely difficult to believe that a belated “purge” has actually scrubbed the data and removed any ancillary…
sentences.zh: 我相信 Palantir 的程度是，它的员工不仅仅是出于个人原因浏览大量传入数据，但我发现很难相信迟来的“清除”实际上已经清除了数据并删除了任何辅助......

sentences.en: well, let’s call them “infections.”
sentences.zh: 好吧，我们称其为“感染”。

sentences.en: Without turning over evidence of this purge to the courts, it’s easy to say it’s all been handled, even if the only thing that happened what Palantir deleting the source CSVs (or whatever) from its system, which isn’t the same thing as stripping it from Palantir’s databases.
sentences.zh: 在不向法院移交此次清除的证据的情况下，很容易说这一切都已得到处理，即使唯一发生的事情是 Palantir 从其系统中删除源 CSV（或其他内容），这与从 Palantir 数据库中删除它不是一回事。

blocks.html_en: Another reason for high levels of skepticism is this: ICE somehow couldn’t stop itself from passing this illegally obtained data to Palantir despite (apparently) trying to comply with a court order.

sentences.en: Another reason for high levels of skepticism is this: ICE somehow couldn’t stop itself from passing this illegally obtained data to Palantir despite (apparently) trying to comply with a court order.
sentences.zh: 高度怀疑的另一个原因是：尽管（显然）试图遵守法院命令，ICE 却无法阻止自己将这些非法获得的数据传递给 Palantir。

blocks.html_en: In a court filing last week, the Justice Department said that CMS again inadvertently reshared with ICE the dataset with millions of names that CMS had first improperly shared with ICE in January. The government said the error occurred during an effort to share data from states not involved in the lawsuit.

sentences.en: In a court filing last week, the Justice Department said that CMS again inadvertently reshared with ICE the dataset with millions of names that CMS had first improperly shared with ICE in January.
sentences.zh: 在上周的一份法庭文件中，司法部表示，CMS 再次无意中与 ICE 重新共享了 CMS 在 1 月份首次与 ICE 不当共享的包含数百万个姓名的数据集。

sentences.en: The government said the error occurred during an effort to share data from states not involved in the lawsuit.
sentences.zh: 政府表示，在分享未参与诉讼的各州的数据时发生了错误。

blocks.html_en: In a court filing last week, the Justice Department said that CMS again inadvertently reshared with ICE the dataset with millions of names that CMS had first improperly shared with ICE in January. The government said the error occurred during an effort to share data from states not involved in the lawsuit.

sentences.en: The government said the error occurred during an effort to share data from states not involved in the lawsuit.
sentences.zh: 政府表示，在分享未参与诉讼的各州的数据时发生了错误。

blocks.html_en: You see the problem, right? Because not every state sued over this illegal data collection, ICE continues to collect data that should — at this point — be considered off-limits. The only reason it doesn’t is because some states (you know the ones) have decided they’ll do whatever it takes to ensure the administration gets to keep being openly racist.

sentences.en: You see the problem, right?
sentences.zh: 你看到问题了，对吧？

sentences.en: Because not every state sued over this illegal data collection, ICE continues to collect data that should — at this point — be considered off-limits.
sentences.zh: 由于并非每个州都会因这种非法数据收集行为提起诉讼，因此 ICE 会继续收集目前应被视为禁区的数据。

sentences.en: The only reason it doesn’t is because some states (you know the ones) have decided they’ll do whatever it takes to ensure the administration gets to keep being openly racist.
sentences.zh: 没有这样做的唯一原因是因为一些州（你知道的）已经决定他们将尽一切努力确保政府继续公开种族主义。

blocks.html_en: Consequently, the data sets aren’t being sorted between racist/non-racist (or whatever the SORT term is), which means ICE continues to retain data it’s been ordered to delete and Palantir keeps getting handed data the government isn’t allowed to collect, much less distribute.

sentences.en: Consequently, the data sets aren’t being sorted between racist/non-racist (or whatever the SORT term is), which means ICE continues to retain data it’s been ordered to delete and Palantir keeps getting handed data the government isn’t allowed to collect, much less distribute.
sentences.zh: 因此，数据集不会在种族主义/非种族主义之间进行排序（或者无论排序术语是什么），这意味着 ICE 继续保留被命令删除的数据，而 Palantir 不断获得政府不允许收集的数据，更不用说分发了。

blocks.html_en: Then there’s the ultimate problem. No matter what’s happening here in the lower courts, the administration will continue to push for a resolution from the US Supreme Court. And the odds are about 6-3 that SCOTUS will say the government can do whatever it wants with whatever data it collects, ignoring years of precedent and administrative firewalls that are meant to protect US citizens (and residents) from being abused and surveilled by their government.

sentences.en: Then there’s the ultimate problem.
sentences.zh: 然后就是最终的问题。

sentences.en: No matter what’s happening here in the lower courts, the administration will continue to push for a resolution from the US Supreme Court.
sentences.zh: 无论下级法院发生什么，政府都将继续推动美国最高法院做出解决方案。

sentences.en: And the odds are about 6-3 that SCOTUS will say the government can do whatever it wants with whatever data it collects, ignoring years of precedent and administrative firewalls that are meant to protect US citizens (and residents) from being abused and surveilled by their government.
sentences.zh: 最高法院认为政府可以对其收集的任何数据为所欲为的可能性约为 6 比 3，无视多年来的先例和旨在保护美国公民（和居民）免受政府虐待和监视的行政防火墙。

blocks.html_en:
Filed Under: dhs, ice, mass deportation, medicaid, medical data, trump administration
Companies: palantir

sentences.en: Filed Under: dhs, ice, mass deportation, medicaid, medical data, trump administration Companies: palantir
sentences.zh: 提起下： 国土安全部, 冰, 大规模驱逐出境, 医疗补助, 医疗数据, 特朗普政府 公司: Palantir

content_html_zh:
来自可接受的附带损害部门

特朗普政府持续走向最糟糕的趋势意味着每一份报告似乎都是“我得到了坏消息，我得到了更糟糕的消息”。 由于政府的更多行为在法庭诉讼中曝光，本来就很糟糕的情况变得更加糟糕。

去年 7 月，特朗普政府单方面决定 ICE 应该获取医疗补助数据，其唯一目的是寻找移民以进行逮捕和驱逐。 政府本身已经明确表示了这一点，并表示这些数据将为 ICE 官员提供更好的工具来发现“外星人的位置”。 当然，国土安全部与医疗保险和医疗补助服务中心达成协议，因为这种情况发生了。 政府已经清除了大量的非效忠派，这意味着剩下的人不会进行太多的战斗。

这种要求没有法律依据，所以标题上写着“非法”。 如果这是一个法律要求，我们就不会看到挑战共享这些敏感医疗数据的诉讼，因为法律已经解决了。 具体来说，ICE 希望获得暴露“家庭住址和种族”的医疗补助数据。 不完全是微妙的，但对于这支怪诞的政府来说，一切都不是那么回事。

但这种分享在法庭上受到了质疑，这让坏消息变成了更糟糕的消息。 据 NPR 报道，ICE 不仅可以获取其在法律上无权获取的信息，而且其私人承包商也可以获取：

根据新的法庭文件，在医疗补助官员一月份与移民官员不当分享了数百万人的数据后，ICE 随后与数据分析公司 Palantir 分享了这些数据。 Palantir 运营一款名为 ELITE 的应用程序，ICE 特工使用该应用程序来显示可能被驱逐出境的非公民的地址。 周四，20 多名民主党总检察长提交的一项动议公开了这一消息，他们去年就医疗保险和医疗补助服务中心与 ICE 之间的数据共享协议起诉特朗普政府。

Palantir 对数据的渴望始终如一。 它会接受政府客户选择提供的任何信息，包括非法获得的信息。

Palantir 根据正在进行的诉讼中法庭披露的内容，针对本报道发表了几份声明。

首先，它表示相关数据已被“清除”。 如果这是真的那就太好了，但这似乎在信任之前需要验证，因为谁知道在法院命令阻止政府使用这些数据之前，这些数据集最终去了哪里。 如果你认为只有 ICE 正在窥视这些非法获取的数据，那么你就给予了政府良好的信誉，但政府不仅没有赢得这种诚意，而且自特朗普当选以来几乎每一分钟都在积极破坏。

ICE 的监控技术承包商也表示：

Palantir 向 NPR 提供了以下声明：“我们的客户控制自己的数据并管理对该数据的访问。 当 Palantir 员工被授予访问客户数据集的权限时，只是为了帮助集成和分析该数据（这就是我们软件的作用），而不是为了我们自己的目的而存储或使用它。 Palantir 可以确认相关数据集已根据政府指示被清除。”

出色地… 我愿意相信这一点，就像我愿意相信制衡体系能够限制流氓政权一样，更不用说不受这些特定限制的私人承包商了。

我相信 Palantir 的程度是，它的员工不仅仅是出于个人原因浏览大量传入数据，但我发现很难相信迟来的“清除”实际上已经清除了数据并删除了任何辅助...... 好吧，我们称其为“感染”。 在不向法院移交此次清除的证据的情况下，很容易说这一切都已得到处理，即使唯一发生的事情是 Palantir 从其系统中删除源 CSV（或其他内容），这与从 Palantir 数据库中删除它不是一回事。

高度怀疑的另一个原因是：尽管（显然）试图遵守法院命令，ICE 却无法阻止自己将这些非法获得的数据传递给 Palantir。

在上周的一份法庭文件中，司法部表示，CMS 再次无意中与 ICE 重新共享了 CMS 在 1 月份首次与 ICE 不当共享的包含数百万个姓名的数据集。 政府表示，在分享未参与诉讼的各州的数据时发生了错误。

你看到问题了，对吧？ 由于并非每个州都会因这种非法数据收集行为提起诉讼，因此 ICE 会继续收集目前应被视为禁区的数据。 没有这样做的唯一原因是因为一些州（你知道的）已经决定他们将尽一切努力确保政府继续公开种族主义。

因此，数据集不会在种族主义/非种族主义之间进行排序（或者无论排序术语是什么），这意味着 ICE 继续保留被命令删除的数据，而 Palantir 不断获得政府不允许收集的数据，更不用说分发了。

然后就是最终的问题。 无论下级法院发生什么，政府都将继续推动美国最高法院做出解决方案。 最高法院认为政府可以对其收集的任何数据为所欲为的可能性约为 6 比 3，无视多年来的先例和旨在保护美国公民（和居民）免受政府虐待和监视的行政防火墙。

提起下： 国土安全部, 冰, 大规模驱逐出境, 医疗补助, 医疗数据, 特朗普政府 公司: Palantir
